paper_title: Mass Spectrometer Analysis of Wood Samples

methods.description: This method involves the analysis of wood samples using continuous-flow-isotope-ratio mass spectrometry to determine isotopic ratios and elemental concentrations. It is used to analyze the isotopic composition of sulfur in wood samples.

steps.description: All samples were analysed by continuous-flow-isotope-ratio mass spectrometry using an Isoprime 100 mass spectrometer linked to an Elementar Pyrocube analyser at the University of Lancaster, Lancaster Environment Centre.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze samples using Isoprime 100 mass spectrometer

steps.description: Combustion of 30e40 mg of wood powder in tin capsules at 1030 C and 70 s oxygen pulse, yielded product N2, CO2 and SO2 for analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: combust wood powder to produce N2, CO2, and SO2

steps.description: CO2 and SO2 gases were trapped using a Thermal Programmed Desorption (TPD) system inherent to the Elementar pyrocube.
steps.searchable_summary: trap CO2 and SO2 using TPD system

steps.description: Capture and focus of product SO2 within the TPD allowed determination of 34S/32S (expressed as d34S) where whole-wood sulphur contents were approximately 0.01%.
d34S values were corrected to V-CDT (Vienna-Canyon Diablo Troilite) using international standards NBS-127 and SO5.
steps.searchable_summary: correct d34S values using international standards

steps.description: Within-run replication of international standards and in-house BaSO4 gave d34S precisions of <0.3& (1 Standard Deviation, analysed in batches of n = 4).

methods.description: This method validates the direct EA method of wood powder analysis by comparing it with offline combustion using Eschka's reagent. It is used to ensure the accuracy of sulfur isotopic analysis.

steps.description: 5 g aliquots of wood powder were mixed with Eschka's reagent in porcelain crucibles and combusted in a muffle furnace at 850 C for two hours.
steps.searchable_summary: combust wood powder with Eschka's reagent

steps.description: The combusted product was rinsed with de-ionised water and filtered to remove solid residues.
The eluent was acidified to pH 2 using HCl and boiled to remove any remaining dissolved inorganic carbon.
Sulphate was precipitated as barium sulphate with the addition of excess barium chloride.
Repeated centrifugation and washing with de-ionised water concentrated the product barium sulphate for analysis.
Barium sulphate produced as an end product of the Eschka digestion was analysed by EA and found to be comparable to those analyses of the same material undertaken by direct EA combustion.
